interpretation_summary: The replication aimed to test whether memorization moderates the Big-Fish-Little-Pond Effect (BFLPE) on mathematics self-concept using PISA 2012 data, mirroring the original (PISA 2003) multilevel approach with plausible values and weights. However, execution failed due to the R environment not exposing Rscript in the container PATH (exit code 127). No models were estimated and no outputs were generated, so no empirical comparison to the original coefficients was possible.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Planned methods closely aligned with the preregistration: three-level multilevel models (students within schools within countries), plausible values pooled across five imputations, standardized variables, student weights, and testing the interaction MEMOR × school-average ability. The replication intentionally used PISA 2012 as an independent-wave robustness test. Alignment cannot be verified in practice because the code did not execute.

deviations.issue_description: Execution environment lacked Rscript in PATH; analysis script could not run (exit code 127).
deviations.impact: High: Prevented any estimation or result generation.

deviations.issue_description: Replication wave differs from original (2012 vs 2003), as planned.
deviations.impact: Medium (if executed): Potential differences in instrument properties and country composition could change effect sizes and SEs.

deviations.issue_description: Planned omission of PISA replicate weights/jackknife in SE estimation.
deviations.impact: Medium (if executed): SEs and p-values might be biased; direction and point estimates typically robust, but uncertainty may be misestimated.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: In PISA 2012, the interaction between students’ use of memorization and school-average mathematics ability is negative in its association with standardized mathematics self-concept.
results_comparison.original_results: Memorization × school-average ability: coefficient ≈ -0.089, p < 0.001; reported effect size ≈ -0.157 (negative moderation of the BFLPE).
results_comparison.replication_results: No estimates produced. Execution failed with exit code 127 due to missing Rscript; no model outputs or pooled results were generated.
results_comparison.overall_answer: No. The replication did not execute; preregistered comparison criteria could not be assessed.

replication_report: Replication failed: No statistical results were produced due to an execution environment error (Rscript not found). Consequently, the negative moderation of school-average ability by memorization on mathematics self-concept could not be tested on PISA 2012.

failure_handling.suggestions: Use a base image with Rscript available (e.g., rocker/verse) and verify PATH (/usr/local/bin/Rscript). As fallback, call R -e 'source("script.R")' or include a shebang (#!/usr/bin/env Rscript) with executable permissions. Pre-install required R packages in the Dockerfile and validate by running a smoke test (sessionInfo(), library checks) before the full model.

Add intermediate checkpoints that save small diagnostic outputs (dataset summaries, N after filters, model formulas, and per-PV quick OLS/lmer fits on a 1% sample) to confirm pipeline health. Provide precomputed outputs or a small synthetic dataset with expected outputs for CI tests.

If/when executed, incorporate PISA replicate weights/jackknife for SEs, or clearly note inference limitations. Ensure pooling across plausible values follows OECD guidelines and report both pooled estimates and uncertainty.

Ship a lightweight subset (few countries/schools) for pipeline verification, along with a variable dictionary and consistent missing-code handling. Confirm adequate memory and set conservative random-effects structures to avoid convergence issues.

notes: Original benchmark: negative interaction (coef ≈ -0.089, p < 0.001; effect size ≈ -0.157). The replication intentionally uses PISA 2012; wave differences and omission of replicate weights would primarily affect SEs/p-values and possibly magnitude if executed. The planned random-effects structure is complex and may require simplification for stability on large N. Recommend staged execution (subset then full sample) after environment fix.
